NCT04960202.name: EPIC-HR
NCT04960202.group: High-risk non-hospitalised COVID-19 adults, nirmatrelvir-ritonavir 300/100 mg BID x5d vs placebo (start <=3 d of symptom onset)

allOutcomes.title: COVID-19-related hospitalisation or death through Day 28 (primary, mITT1 <=3 d)

NCT04960202.snippet: Source: Hammond J et al. N Engl J Med 2022;386:1397-1408 (EPIC-HR Paxlovid).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 2,246 unvaccinated adults with symptomatic COVID-19, at least one risk factor for severe disease, randomized 1:1 double-blind to nirmatrelvir 300 mg + ritonavir 100 mg orally every 12 hours for 5 days (n=1,120) or matching placebo (n=1,126). Treatment started within 5 days of symptom onset. Primary analysis: mITT1 population treated within 3 days of symptom onset (n=1,379).

evidence.label: Primary - Hospitalisation or Death by Day 28
evidence.text: COVID-19-related hospitalisation or any-cause death through Day 28 (mITT1, treatment within 3 days): nirmatrelvir-ritonavir 5/697 (0.77%) vs placebo 44/682 (6.31%); relative risk 0.11 (95% CI 0.04 to 0.29; P<0.001). 89% relative reduction. No deaths in active arm vs 9 in placebo.

evidence.label: Secondary - Viral Load and Safety
evidence.text: SARS-CoV-2 RNA viral load reduction at Day 5: nirmatrelvir-ritonavir 0.87 log10 copies/mL greater reduction vs placebo (P<0.001). Dysgeusia and diarrhoea more common on active; serious adverse events were not more frequent.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by prior mAb treatment. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 96% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central endpoint adjudication for hospitalisations and deaths. D5 LOW - SAP locked; interim futility/efficacy boundaries crossed, unblinded for efficacy.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection within 5 days prior to randomization~* Initial onset of COVID-19 signs/symptoms within 5 days prior to the day of randomization and at least 1 of the specified COVID-19 signs/symptoms present on the day of randomization~* Fertile participants must agree to use a highly effective method of contraception~* Has at least 1 characteristic or underlying medical condition associated with an increased risk of developing severe illness from COVID-19~E...

NCT04575597.name: MOVe-OUT
NCT04575597.group: High-risk non-hospitalised COVID-19 adults, molnupiravir 800 mg BID x5d vs placebo (start <=5 d of symptom onset)

allOutcomes.title: COVID-19-related hospitalisation or death through Day 29 (primary)

NCT04575597.snippet: Source: Jayk Bernal A et al. N Engl J Med 2022;386:509-520 (MOVe-OUT molnupiravir).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,433 unvaccinated adults with symptomatic mild-to-moderate COVID-19 and at least one risk factor for severe disease randomized 1:1 double-blind to molnupiravir 800 mg orally every 12 hours for 5 days (n=709) or matching placebo (n=699). Treatment started within 5 days of symptom onset.

evidence.label: Primary - Hospitalisation or Death by Day 29
evidence.text: COVID-19-related hospitalisation or any-cause death through Day 29 (all-randomised analysis): molnupiravir 48/709 (6.8%) vs placebo 68/699 (9.7%); relative risk 0.69 (95% CI 0.48 to 0.99). 31% relative reduction. Effect smaller than the pre-specified interim (which had shown 48% reduction in 775-patient subset).

evidence.label: Secondary - Subgroup Signals
evidence.text: Subgroup analysis showed no benefit (or trend towards harm) in vaccinated-but-enrolled subset and in patients with COVID-19 antibodies at baseline. Driving signal was in seronegative, unvaccinated, higher-risk patients.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 95% follow-up. D4 LOW - central adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked; attenuation between interim and final analysis noted and addressed in pre-specified sensitivity analyses.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Has documentation of laboratory confirmed severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection with sample collection ≤5 days prior to the day of randomization. PCR is the preferred method; however with evolving approaches to laboratory confirmation of SARS-CoV-2 infection, other molecular or antigen tests that detect viral ribonucleic acid (RNA) or protein are allowed if authorized for use in the country.
